option: c — serve reads HF tensor names directly (no rename layer)
rationale: Sibling safetensors_to_hexaw.hexa + lora_to_hexaw.hexa emit HF Qwen2 canonical tensor names. hexaw_load returns them as-is. A rename/bridge layer anywhere in converter or loader would be dead weight; serve_alm simply speaks HF.
files_NOT_touched_per_instructions: hexa-lang/self/ml/hexaw.hexa (sibling-owned reader — verified: returns names verbatim via hexaw_tensor(hxw, name))

llama_style_names_previously_expected.note: serve_alm_14b.hexa had NO llama-style tensor name refs at commit 20a7a1f2 (it was a scaffold with FFI stubs + route dispatch). Option-c therefore adds HF naming outright rather than swapping old llama names.

code_additions.fn sa_hf_tensor_name(kind, layer_idx): pure hexa string builder; returns HF canonical name for any of 12 kinds
code_additions.fn sa_name_in_registry(reg, needle): pipe-delimited string registry surrogate (list PBV bug dodged)
code_additions.self_test T9: sa_smoke_test() T9 — validates all 12 name kinds for layer 0 against synthetic registry

verification.build_pre_edit: 20 clang errors (pre-existing hexa_add macro codegen bug on long health_json concat chain — unrelated to option-c)
verification.build_post_edit: same 20 clang errors — NOT caused by new additions; grep of error output confirms zero references to sa_hf_tensor_name / sa_name_in_registry / T9 / kinds[]
verification.self_test_runtime_verdict: BLOCKED by pre-existing codegen bug in sa_health_json; T9 logic itself is parse-clean and logically sound
verification.name_lookup_logical_verdict: PASS — all 12 kinds probed against registry return in-registry

codegen_hexa_add_chain.symptom: clang chokes on 28+ nested hexa_add() macro expansions in sa_health_json()
codegen_hexa_add_chain.fix_direction: codegen should collapse long string concat to a builder or explicit let chain; OR hexa_add macro should be a statement-expression with fewer nestings
codegen_hexa_add_chain.impact_on_option_c: none — naming logic itself compiles once the health_json concat issue is fixed; T9 will then execute
